nodes.claim: A cancer cell is not a broken cell — it has lost context.
nodes.why: The spine. Reframes cancer from machinery-failure to signal-failure: the cell still functions, but inside a model of the world that is no longer true.

nodes.claim: Hallmarks of Cancer (2011) + New Dimensions (2022) — cancer as acquired capabilities that escape tissue signaling and microenvironmental constraint.
nodes.why: Grounds the spine in the canonical cancer-biology frame. The 2011 update foregrounds tumor-promoting inflammation and microenvironment — the bridge the essay builds on.

nodes.claim: Tissue Organization Field Theory — cancer as tissue-level disorder of intercellular constraint.
nodes.why: The minority-position citation that takes the context-loss frame furthest. Pairs with Hanahan-Weinberg to triangulate the spine claim from two independent literatures.

nodes.claim: Open-knowledge-graph — bounded-context framework. Paper 11 (The Ratchet) and Paper 5 (Genome as Projection).
nodes.why: The theoretical scaffolding the essay leans on — cancer and evolution as opposing failures of the same bounded-context mechanism.

nodes.claim: Fascia partitions — the body partitions itself when signal is associated with danger.
nodes.why: The somatic instantiation of the spine. What context-loss looks like in connective tissue: dehydration, adhesion, regions stop participating, force routes around them.

nodes.claim: Fascia hydration and hyaluronan-driven interlayer glide; densification compromises the system.
nodes.source: Schleip et al. (2012, 2015); Stecco et al. (2011)
nodes.why: Grounds the fascia mechanism. The hyaluronan / glide / densification chain is solid in the bodywork literature; the essay borrows it cleanly.

nodes.claim: Restorative yoga reopens the channel — long supported holds let the matrix re-hydrate, adhesions soften, the partitioned region rejoins surrounding signal.
nodes.why: The intervention claim at the fascia layer. Mechanism-level (re-hydration, adhesion-softening), not outcome-level (cure).

nodes.claim: The vagus is the body's listening line — long exhale signals safety; HRV measures its flexibility.
nodes.why: The nervous-system instantiation of the spine. Locked vagus reads alarm; flexible vagus reads context. Same partition dynamic, different tissue.

nodes.claim: Polyvagal theory — vagal tone, supported postures, long exhales, inflammation-vagal-tone correlation. Phylogenetic claims contested by Grossman/Taylor; clinical readouts robust.
nodes.why: Honest citation. Theory is contested at the phylogenetic layer; the load-bearing readouts (HRV, exhale, vagal-tone-inflammation) replicate independently.

nodes.claim: HRV–inflammation meta-analysis — vagally-mediated HRV inversely tracks circulating inflammatory markers via the cholinergic anti-inflammatory pathway.
nodes.source: Williams et al., Brain, Behavior, and Immunity (2019)
nodes.why: The bridge citation. Provides the HRV-to-inflammation half of the synthesis; the inflammation-to-cancer-permissive half comes from Hanahan-Weinberg. Neither paper makes the full bridge.

nodes.claim: A cancer cell is a cell that has lost the signal that says: you are held.
nodes.why: The full synthesis. Connects hallmarks-of-cancer to the nervous-system / fascia frame — same partition dynamic, three tissues. Not a cure claim; a frame claim.

eyes.what: The HRV → inflammation → cancer-permissive bridge is the author's synthesis across three literatures, not a cited consensus. The footnote names this explicitly; flagging here for transparency.
eyes.why: Williams 2019 covers HRV-to-inflammation; Hanahan-Weinberg names tumor-promoting inflammation as enabling characteristic. No single paper closes the loop. Worth empirical follow-up.

ground_with.label: Hanahan & Weinberg 2011 — tumor-promoting inflammation as enabling characteristic

eyes.what: "Restorative yoga cannot cure cancer" is the disclaimer. The positive claim — reopening the partition channel — is mechanism-level, not outcome-level.
eyes.why: Mechanism plausibility (matrix re-hydration, adhesion softening) draws on mechanical-loading studies, not restorative-yoga trials. Some oncology supportive-care trials of yoga exist; worth citing if findable.

ground_with.label: Yoga in oncology supportive care — supportive-care trials

eyes.what: The bridge from "cell-autonomous capabilities" (Hanahan-Weinberg) to "cell that lost the signal you are held" is poetic synthesis, not a direct mapping in the source.
eyes.why: The frame is load-bearing for the essay. The 2011 + 2022 hallmarks updates do foreground microenvironment and inflammation, which makes the bridge defensible — but the affective register ("held") is the author's addition, not the literature's.

eyes.what: The fascia mechanism (hydration, glide, densification) is solid for mechanical-loading contexts. The bridge from "long supported holds" to "matrix re-hydrates" is mechanism-plausible but not directly observed in restorative-yoga studies.
eyes.why: Schleip + Stecco grounds the tissue mechanics; the specific intervention pathway (long supported holds → ANS-mediated viscosity change → re-hydration) is inferred from Schleip Part 2 (ANS-mediated viscosity changes), not directly trialed.

actions.label: Long Exhale
actions.body: 4-in / 8-out for two minutes. Drills D03 — the vagus reading safety from breath shape.

actions.label: Tending the Ember
actions.body: Full restorative routine. Long supported holds at the fascial layer. Drills D01 + D02.

actions.label: Coming Home
actions.body: Full restorative routine. Re-establishes the broadcast across nervous system and fascia.

actions.label: The Open Sky
actions.body: Full restorative routine. Sustained witnessed stillness — the closer's "you are held" at body scale.

actions.label: Find a partitioned region
actions.body: Locate one fascial region in your body that has stopped participating — chronic shoulder, hip, ribcage. Spend 90 seconds in supported child's pose with breath at 4:8 ratio. Notice the local re-hydration cue.

actions.label: Map sympathetic dominance
actions.body: Walk through your day: where does sympathetic dominance climb? Where could you stack a 2-minute long-exhale break? The vagus listens to whatever cadence you teach it.

actions.label: Same partition pattern, different scale
actions.body: Know-thyself Part I argues the same shape at memory scale — flat memory partitions claims from their evidence the way braced fascia partitions tissue from signal.
